title: Santalodes frutescens
taxonomy: Santalodes frutescens is classified as a species of plant within the family Santalaceae, commonly known as the sandalwood family. This family comprises approximately 42 genera and around 1,000 species of plants. The genus Santalodes is relatively small, with only a few species. The classification of Santalodes frutescens is further broken down into its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiosperms), and class (Magnoliopsida). The specific classification is still a topic of ongoing research and may be subject to revision.

intro: Santalodes frutescens, also known as White Sandalwood, is a plant species belonging to the Santalaceae family, characterized by its fragrant and aromatic properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Santalodes frutescens include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal and aromatic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Santalodes frutescens is often used to make essential oils, extracts, and teas, as well as being incorporated into skincare products and aromatherapy blends.
